channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump Treasury confirms it's eyeing IRS for 'streamlining' shake-up as tax season heats up
item.description: The Trump administration's Treasury Department is considering a "wide range" of possibilities to streamline the tax agency, Fox News Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration’s plans to cut the VA’s budget
item.description: A handful of veterans speaking to The Associated Press are divided over the Trump administration’s proposed cuts to the Department of Veterans Affairs. Stephen Watson is a former Marine who lives in Jesup, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Snap polls reveal how speech watchers reacted to Trump's address to Congress
item.description: Most people who watched President Donald Trump's primetime address to a joint session of Congress had a positive opinion of what he spelled out in speech, according to snap polls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith responds to Trump address, looming government shutdown
item.description: The Minnesota Democrat dissented along with her caucus. She joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer on Wednesday to talk about the president’s speech promoting his administration’s accomplishments so far.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Vance visits the US-Mexico border to tout Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: EAGLE PASS, Texas (AP) — Vice President JD Vance is visiting the U.S.-Mexico border on Wednesday to highlight the tougher immigration policies that the White House says has led to dramatically fewer arrests for illegal crossings since Donald Trump began his second term.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Buttigieg says he's 'looking at' possible Senate run in battleground state
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary and former Democratic presidential candidate Pete Buttigieg says he's "looking at" a possible run in 2026 for an open Senate seat in battleground Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia Democrat Lucy McBath says she's exploring a 2026 run for governor
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia U.S. Rep. Lucy McBath filed paperwork on Wednesday to raise money for a run for governor in 2026, becoming the first Democrat to take that step. McBath described her effort as an exploratory committee, although that could quickly become a full-blown campaign.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Sylvester Turner, first-term Democrat, dies at 70
item.description: Rep. Sylvester Turner (D-Texas), the former Houston mayor who won a seat in Congress in November, succeeding the late Rep. Sheila Jackson Lee (D), died Tuesday night. He was 70. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) confirmed Turner’s death Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats behavior presented party as 'hateful': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly says the behavior from the Democrats was 'hateful' as they did not acknowledge things presented by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court reinstates order requiring Trump administration to release frozen foreign aid
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected a Trump administration push to rebuke a federal judge who imposed a quick deadline to release billions of dollars in foreign aid.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After Trump speech, AOC tells Democrats to organize in GOP districts against Medicaid cuts
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., is calling on Democrats to organize in GOP-heavy districts to push back against the Trump agenda and what she claims will be drastic cuts to Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vows to press ahead on reshaping America in speech to Congress as Democrats register dissent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy in an unyielding address before Congress that left Democratic legislators to register their dissent with stone faces, placards
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump addresses joint session of Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says America would welcome Greenland during joint address to Congress
item.description: During his address before a joint session of Congress, President Donald Trump continued pushing the idea of Greenland becoming part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's address to Congress showed the country's stark partisan divide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy wants peace, ready to accept minerals deal after WH blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy had written to him to say he appreciates U.S. support for his country in its war with Russia and is ready to sign a deal that could ensure future American support.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Kabul airport bombing during Afghanistan pullout has been captured and is due in US court
item.description: A suspected senior planner in the suicide bombing at the Kabul airport that killed 13 American service members and roughly 170 Afghan civilians during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan has been captured and will appear in federal court in the United States to face charges.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump clashes with Democrats at testy joint address
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday sparred with Democratic lawmakers during an address to a joint session of Congress that began with a congressman being escorted out of the chamber and saw other Democrats leave the room as Trump spoke.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fired federal workers are among the guests invited to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: The Trumps and lawmakers from across the aisle have invited guests to Tuesday's joint address, representing some of the administration's top priorities. Here's a look at some of the names on the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: In hearing, Texas Democrats criticize school leaders for being too “nice” about their funding needs
item.description: Lawmakers on Tuesday discussed House Bill 2, which would raise schools’ base funding per student by $220 — a figure that falls short of what many had sought previously.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Dan Goldman looks to be 'the man in the arena' against Trump
item.description: Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) wasn’t planning to run for Congress after his stint as the lead counsel on President Trump’s first impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominee to lead National Institutes of Health questioned on funding cuts, vaccines
item.description: President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the National Institutes of Health is promising to help restore trust in medical research.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In polarized times, these Minnesota legislators want to restore civility at the state Capitol
item.description: This year’s legislative session started with a lot of vitriol between DFLers and Republicans. But a bipartisan group of legislators is trying to get both sides to be more collaborative and civil.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Billions in federal funding at stake for sanctuary cities
item.description: The mayors of Denver, Chicago, Boston and New York will appear before a GOP-led committee to discuss sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Cuomo takes center stage in race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The race for New York City mayor is now officially revolving around former Gov. Andrew Cuomo following his declaration over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: 3 things in Minnesota that will be impacted by tariffs on Canada
item.description: Tariffs from the Trump administration are set to go in effect on Tuesday. Canada and Minnesota do over $21 billion in trade every year.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Ellen Finn, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada lawmakers told Medicaid cuts could mean huge budget hole, widespread loss of benefits
item.description: With a key budget hearing on Medicaid funding scheduled on Thursday, Nevada lawmakers worked Wednesday to be prepared if federal cuts pose a threat to the state budget.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa Bay families travel to Capitol Hill to urge lawmakers for more pediatric cancer funding
item.description: Embracing photos and wearing the names of their children who were taken too soon, families took off from Tampa International Airport Wednesday morning, headed for D.C. to urge lawmakers to pass more funding for pediatric cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC
